title: Painful left bundle branch block syndrome: A case report.

sections.title: Painful left bundle branch block syndrome: A case report.
sections.text: The painful left bundle branch block (LBBB) syndrome, a rare and often underdiagnosed condition, remains a significant challenge in clinical cardiology. In this case report, we present a 74-year-old female patient who exhibited classic features of rate-dependent LBBB accompanied by angina pectoris. Intriguingly, comprehensive diagnostic evaluations revealed no evidence of obstructive coronary artery disease, highlighting the complex pathophysiology underlying this unique clinical presentation.
